Package Character_Classes

Class PlayableChar

java.lang.Object
Character_Classes.GameCharacter
Character_Classes.PlayableChar
public class PlayableChar extends GameCharacter
Represents a user-controlled character within the game. Tracks character statistics including inventory management, currency tracking, step counts, item usage, tile interactions, and dynamic combat outcomes.
Author:
Katigbak and Porciuncula

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from class Character_Classes.GameCharacter

    attack, dialogue, health, name, x, y

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    PlayableChar(String name, float health, float attack, String dialogue)
    Constructs a playable character with defined statistics and resets status variables to their starting configurations.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addItem(Item i)
    Appends an item to the character's active inventory array.
    boolean
    buyItem(Item purchase)
    Facilitates purchasing an item asset if fiscal gold limits allow.
    void
    collect(DestructibleTile tile, Floor floor)
    Extracts items or currency value arrays directly out of destructible tile types, updating internal inventory and purging map structures afterwards.
    void

    Executes wall destruction processing against blocked target map coordinates.
    boolean
    discardItem(int index)
    Discards an item asset out of the active list using an explicit collection index.
    void
    findCharTile(Tile[][] map)
    Scans the 2D layout grid structure looking for explicitly matching unique character keys to accurately synchronize tracking coordinates during initialization steps.

    Increments the total running turn engine index step count by one.

    move(char direction, Floor floor)
    Implements step direction handling ('w','a','s','d'), combat conflict discovery, hazard step environmental damage logging, and destructible path routing interactions.

    Iterates forward through the inventory layout to equip the next item node.
    void

    Unlocks a treasure tile to distribute environmental asset drops.

    takeDmg(GameCharacter entity)
    Overrides standard damage reception to apply defensive subtraction and logging tracking enemy names if the strike results in a character defeat.

    useItem()
    Triggers the active function of the selected item, handles its depletion removal, re-indexes standard item assignments, and steps the engine turn metrics.

  • Constructor Details

    • PlayableChar

      public PlayableChar(String name, float health, float attack, String dialogue)
      Constructs a playable character with defined statistics and resets status variables to their starting configurations.
      Parameters:
      name - the identifier name of the playable character
      health - the starting health and ceiling threshold capacity
      attack - the base attack damage capacity
      dialogue - the interaction line text

  • Method Details

    • getGoldOwned

      public int getGoldOwned()
      Returns:
      the total amount of gold owned

    • setGoldOwned

      public void setGoldOwned(int goldOwned)
      Parameters:
      goldOwned - the updated gold value to assign

    • getMaxHealth

      public float getMaxHealth()
      Returns:
      the upper limit ceiling of character health

    • setMaxHealth

      public void setMaxHealth(int maxHealth)
      Parameters:
      maxHealth - the new upper threshold for health

    • getTurnCount

      public int getTurnCount()
      Returns:
      the running record tracking total moves executed

    • setTurnCount

      public void setTurnCount(int turnCount)
      Parameters:
      turnCount - the custom integer turn step index

    • addItem

      public void addItem(Item i)
      Appends an item to the character's active inventory array.
      Parameters:
      i - the Item asset to insert

    • getInventory

      public ArrayList<Item> getInventory()
      Returns:
      the full list structure tracking held items

    • setInventory

      public void setInventory(ArrayList<Item> inventory)
      Parameters:
      inventory - the new list allocation replacing the current data

    • getCurItem

      public Item getCurItem()
      Returns:
      the item currently targeted for upcoming action steps

    • setCurItem

      public void setCurItem(Item curItem)
      Parameters:
      curItem - the item node to swap selection toward

    • getCauseOfDeath

      public String getCauseOfDeath()
      Returns:
      the identifier string explaining what defeated the character

    • incrementTurn

      public void incrementTurn()
      Increments the total running turn engine index step count by one.

    • takeDmg

      public void takeDmg(GameCharacter entity)
      Overrides standard damage reception to apply defensive subtraction and logging tracking enemy names if the strike results in a character defeat.
      Parameters:
      entity - the attacking entity dealing the blow

    • prevItem

      public boolean prevItem()
      Iterates backward through the inventory layout to equip the previous item node.
      Returns:
      true if selection changed successfully, false if structure is too small

    • nextItem

      public boolean nextItem()
      Iterates forward through the inventory layout to equip the next item node.
      Returns:
      true if selection changed successfully, false if structure is too small

    • useItem

      public boolean useItem()
      Triggers the active function of the selected item, handles its depletion removal, re-indexes standard item assignments, and steps the engine turn metrics.
      Returns:
      true if item was successfully validated and used, false otherwise

    • buyItem

      public boolean buyItem(Item purchase)
      Facilitates purchasing an item asset if fiscal gold limits allow.
      Parameters:
      purchase - the Item targeted for purchase
      Returns:
      true if bought successfully, false otherwise

    • discardItem

      public boolean discardItem(int index)
      Discards an item asset out of the active list using an explicit collection index.
      Parameters:
      index - the coordinate index of the target object node
      Returns:
      true if dropped successfully, false otherwise

    • heal

      public boolean heal(float amount)
      Applies restorative values to the active health variable pool without surpassing the defined maximum capability limit.
      Parameters:
      amount - the numerical health value to add
      Returns:
      true if recovery processes successfully, false if value is invalid

    • dig

      public void dig(Tile tile, Floor floor)
      Executes wall destruction processing against blocked target map coordinates.
      Parameters:
      tile - the blocking target tile coordinate component
      floor - the current Floor context to mutate

    • collect

      public void collect(DestructibleTile tile, Floor floor)
      Extracts items or currency value arrays directly out of destructible tile types, updating internal inventory and purging map structures afterwards.
      Parameters:
      tile - the target source collectible tile
      floor - the active Floor map context modification reference

    • openTreasure

      public void openTreasure(DestructibleTile tile)
      Unlocks a treasure tile to distribute environmental asset drops.
      Parameters:
      tile - the target treasure-chest element layout

    • move

      public void move(char direction, Floor floor)
      Implements step direction handling ('w','a','s','d'), combat conflict discovery, hazard step environmental damage logging, and destructible path routing interactions.
      Parameters:
      direction - the character character layout input indicating path target
      floor - the target Floor system execution reference

    • findCharTile

      public void findCharTile(Tile[][] map)
      Scans the 2D layout grid structure looking for explicitly matching unique character keys to accurately synchronize tracking coordinates during initialization steps.
      Parameters:
      map - the target tile layout matrix structure to process
